list.title: atomBBox
list.description: Axis-aligned bounding box of a set of atoms with 2D coordinates.

list.title: atomColor
list.description: Returns the CPK fill colour for an element symbol.

list.title: getAtomLabel
list.description: Returns the display label for an atom in 2D skeletal notation, or null for unlabelled carbons. The H-count subscript is placed left or right of the element symbol based on the average neighbour direction.

list.title: labelHalfH
list.description: Half-height of an atom label bounding box in SVG pixels.

list.title: labelHalfW
list.description: Half-width of an atom label bounding box in SVG pixels.

list.title: labelTextOffset
list.description: Returns the horizontal shift applied to the rendered text box so the element symbol stays centered on the atom while any attached H fragment extends to the chosen side.

list.title: module:algorithms/aromaticity.refreshAromaticity
list.description:
Clears stale aromatic flags/bond orders and re-perceives aromaticity.

This is intended for graph edits where an originally aromatic system may have been broken. Callers should ensure any aromatic bonds that need a recoverable Kekule assignment already carry localizedOrder (for example by calling kekulize(mol) beforehand).

list.title: module:algorithms/aromaticity~_isHuckel
list.description: Returns true if piCount satisfies Hückel's rule (4n + 2, n ≥ 0).

list.title: module:algorithms/aromaticity~_kekulizeStale
list.description:
Assigns localizedOrder (1 or 2) to bonds that are currently flagged aromatic but do NOT belong to any confirmed aromatic ring. This covers cases where the SMILES parser used lowercase atoms for a ring which Hückel analysis rejected (e.g. the pyrimidinone ring in hypoxanthine written as O=c1nc[nH]c2[nH]cnc12). Without this step those bonds would all be reset to order 1, losing the C=N double bond character.

The algorithm is a simplified maximum-matching identical in logic to the kekulize function in layout/mol2d-helpers, restricted to the stale atoms.

list.title: module:algorithms/aromaticity~_piElectrons
list.description:
Counts the π electrons contributed by atom when it is part of ringAtomSet.

Rules (Hückel):

  • C (neutral, participates in ring double bond): 1
  • C+ (carbocation): 0
  • C- (carbanion): 2
  • N pyridine-like (no H, one ring double bond): 1
  • N pyrrole-like (has H, lone pair into ring): 2
  • O / S (lone pair into ring): 2
  • B (empty p orbital): 0
  • Already-marked aromatic atom (order=1.5): 1 (each contributes 1 to the 4n+2 sum)

list.title: module:algorithms/morgan.morganRanks
list.description:
Assigns canonical atom ranks to the heavy atoms of mol using a Morgan-style extended-connectivity algorithm with tie-breaking.

Based on Weininger (1989) canonical SMILES paper. Works on a single connected component; for multi-component molecules call per-component.

Initial invariant tuple per atom: [atomic_number, heavy_degree, charge, isotope_mass, bonded_H_count, is_aromatic]

After convergence, any remaining ties (symmetric atoms) are broken deterministically: within each tied group the lex-min initial-invariant atom is ranked first, then Morgan is re-run until stability. Repeating this until all ranks are unique guarantees a fully canonical ordering.

list.title: module:algorithms/morgan.morganRanks~extendRanks
list.description: One round of Morgan extension: extend each atom's invariant with the sorted neighbor ranks.

list.title: module:algorithms/morgan~assignRanks
list.description: Given an array of invariant arrays (one per atom), returns an integer rank array where rank 0 is the lex-smallest invariant and tied atoms share the same rank.

list.title: module:algorithms/morgan~lexCmp
list.description: Lexicographically compares two arrays of numbers. Returns negative / 0 / positive like Array.sort's comparator.

list.title: module:algorithms/paths.allPairsShortestPaths
list.description:
Computes the all-pairs shortest-path distance matrix for a molecule using BFS.

Returns a 2D array D where D[i][j] is the shortest path length between atom i and atom j. Atoms are indexed in insertion order.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.defaultAtomMatch
list.description: Default atom match: element symbol, formal charge, aromaticity, and explicit radical count must all be equal.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.defaultBondMatch
list.description: Default bond match: aromatic flag and bond order must be equal. Aromatic bonds (aromatic === true) are compared on aromaticity alone; their stored order (1.5) is not compared separately.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.elementOnlyAtomMatch
list.description: Element-only atom match — only the element symbol is compared. Charge and aromaticity are ignored. Intended for MCS where scaffold topology matters but ionisation state does not.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.makeAtomMatcher
list.description:
Creates an atom-match predicate from a set of optional constraints.

Supported constraint keys:

  • element {string} — element symbol must equal this value
  • charge {number} — formal charge must equal this value
  • aromatic {boolean} — aromaticity flag must equal this value

Any key that is absent (or undefined) is treated as a wildcard for that property. An empty constraints object returns a wildcard matcher.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.makeBondMatcher
list.description:
Creates a bond-match predicate from a set of optional constraints.

Supported constraint keys:

  • order {number} — bond order must equal this value (ignored when aromatic is true)
  • aromatic {boolean} — aromaticity flag must equal this value

An empty constraints object returns a wildcard matcher.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.wildcardAtomMatch
list.description: Wildcard atom match — any atom matches any other atom. Useful as a building block for SMARTS * patterns and MCS.

list.title: module:algorithms/subgraph.wildcardBondMatch
list.description: Wildcard bond match — any bond matches any other bond. Useful for SMARTS ~ (any bond) and MCS bond-insensitive matching.

list.title: module:algorithms/traversal.bfs
list.description: Performs a breadth-first search starting from the given atom.

list.title: module:algorithms/traversal.dfs
list.description: Performs a depth-first search starting from the given atom.

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2.findFirstSubgraphMapping
list.description: Returns the first mapping from query into target, or null if none exists. Equivalent to consuming the first value from findSubgraphMappings(target, query, { ...options, limit: 1 }).

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2.findSubgraphMappings
list.description:
Generator that yields every injective mapping from query atoms into target atoms that satisfies the given compatibility predicates.

Each yielded value is a Map<queryAtomId, targetAtomId>. The generator is lazy: pulling one value with generator.next() or using { limit: 1 } in the options terminates as soon as the first match is found.

Options:

  • atomMatch (qAtom, tAtom) => boolean — defaults to exact element/charge/aromatic match
  • bondMatch (qBond, tBond) => boolean — defaults to exact order/aromatic match
  • limit number — stop after this many mappings (default: Infinity)
  • skipElementFilter boolean — disable the O(n) element-frequency pre-filter (required when using wildcard or SMARTS atom predicates)

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2.matchesSubgraph
list.description: Returns true if query is isomorphic to some subgraph of target.

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2~_buildIndex
list.description: Builds an O(V+E) adjacency index for a molecule. Constructed once per findSubgraphMappings call for both the query and target; not cached on the Molecule instance (which is mutable).

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2~_candidates
list.description:
Returns candidate target atom IDs for mapping queryOrder[depth].

When targetFrontier is non-empty (atoms adjacent to the already-mapped target subgraph), only frontier atoms are considered — this is the VF2 connectivity constraint that keeps the search space small. When the frontier is empty (first atom, or disconnected query graph) all unmapped target atoms are candidates.

Degree filter: a query atom of degree d can only map to a target atom with degree ≥ d (subgraph, not induced-subgraph semantics).

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2~_elementFrequencyOk
list.description: Returns false when the target provably cannot contain the query because it lacks sufficient atoms of some element. Runs in O(distinct elements in query). Only valid when atomMatch === defaultAtomMatch (exact element matching); pass skipElementFilter: true for wildcards.

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2~_feasible
list.description:
Tests whether extending the current mapping with (qId → tId) is locally consistent:

  1. Atom labels must be compatible (atomMatch).
  2. For every already-mapped neighbour qNb of qId, its image tNb must be adjacent to tId, and the bond (tId, tNb) must be compatible with (qId, qNb).

list.title: module:algorithms/vf2~_queryOrder
list.description:
Returns query atom IDs sorted for optimal VF2 performance.

Strategy: start BFS from the highest-degree atom (largest branching factor eliminated first); within each BFS level sort by degree descending (maximises look-ahead pruning effectiveness). Disconnected components are appended after, also sorted by degree.

list.title: module:app/core/undo.initUndo
list.description: Inject shared state accessors. Call once after simulation and g are created.

list.title: module:app/render/reaction-2d._isBondHighlighted
list.description: Returns true if id1 and id2 belong to the same SMARTS match instance.

list.title: module:app/render/reaction-2d._setHighlight
list.description: Apply (or clear) a highlight from a list of SMARTS mappings and redraw.

list.title: module:app/ui/export.initExport
list.description: Inject shared state references. Call once after g and simulation are created.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#_nextId
list.description: Monotonically increasing counter used for auto-generated IDs.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#bonds
list.description: Bond IDs connected to this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#computeCharge
list.description:
Computes the formal charge from element identity and total bond order.

  • Groups 1–2 (s-block): neutral valence = group (H=1, Be=2, …)
  • Groups 3–12 (transition metals): returns 0 (valence is too variable)
  • Groups 13–17, period 2 (p-block, row 2): neutral valence = 18 − group
  • Groups 13–17, period 3+ (p-block, row 3+): expanded valence — finds the smallest same-parity valence ≥ totalBondOrder, capped at base+4. e.g. S (base=2, cap=6): accepts 2, 4, or 6 bonds; P (base=3, cap=7): 3, 5, or 7.

formal charge = totalBondOrder − effective neutral valence

Returns 0 when group is not set.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getAtomMap
list.description: Returns the stored reaction atom-map number, or null when unset.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getCharge
list.description: Returns the explicit formal charge stored on this atom. Convenience accessor for this.properties.charge.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getChirality
list.description: Returns the CIP chirality designation stored on this atom: 'R', 'S', or null.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getDegree
list.description: Returns the degree (number of bonds) of this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getHeavyNeighbors
list.description: Returns all heavy-atom (non-hydrogen) neighbours of this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getHybridization
list.description: Returns the stored hybridization assignment, or null when unset.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getHydrogenNeighbors
list.description: Returns all hydrogen neighbours of this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getNeighbors
list.description: Returns all neighbouring Atom instances of this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getRadical
list.description: Returns the explicit radical count stored on this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#getValence
list.description: Returns the sum of all bond orders on this atom (total bond order / valence used).

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#id
list.description: Unique identifier for this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#implicitHydrogenCount
list.description:
Returns the number of implicit hydrogen atoms this atom would bear given its current bonding, without mutating the molecule.

Counts only non-H bond order toward the neutral valence; pendant H atoms already attached are not counted as implicit.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#isAromatic
list.description: Returns true when this atom is marked aromatic.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#isChiralCenter
list.description:
Returns true when this atom is a tetrahedral chiral centre.

Without molecule — returns true when a CIP designation ('R' or 'S') has already been stored on properties.chirality (e.g. by parseSMILES).

With molecule — performs a full graph-based test regardless of whether chirality has been annotated:

  1. The atom must be sp3-like (no double/triple/aromatic bonds).
  2. It must have exactly 4 explicit substituents (counting H atoms).
  3. All 4 CIP priority subtrees must be pairwise distinct.

For best results pass a molecule where hydrogens are explicit (as returned directly by parseSMILES).

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#isInRing
list.description:
Tests whether this atom is part of a ring (cycle) in the molecular graph.

Uses a vertex-cut check: this atom is excluded from traversal, then a BFS is run from one neighbour. If any other neighbour is reachable, the atom lies on at least one cycle.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#isRadical
list.description: Returns true when the atom has one or more explicitly stored radicals.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#isSaturated
list.description:
Returns true when this atom's current bond order meets or exceeds its neutral valence (i.e. no unsatisfied valence remaining).

Uses the same group-based valence rules as _adjustImplicitHydrogens: groups 1–2 → valence = group; groups 13–17 → valence = 18 − group. Returns true for transition metals, noble gases, and unknown elements.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#isTerminal
list.description: Returns true when this atom has exactly one bond (i.e. it is a leaf node in the molecular graph).

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#name
list.description: Element symbol.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#properties
list.description: Chemistry-specific element data.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#resolveElement
list.description: Looks up this.name in the periodic-table data and assigns group, period, protons, neutrons, and electrons to this.properties. No-ops silently if the element symbol is not found in the table.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#setAromatic
list.description: Sets the aromatic flag on this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#setAtomMap
list.description: Sets the reaction atom-map number stored on this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#setCharge
list.description: Sets the formal charge and derives the electron count as electrons = protons − charge. If protons is not yet set (manually-built atom), electrons is left unchanged.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#setChirality
list.description:
Sets the CIP chirality designation on this atom.

When molecule is provided, setting 'R' or 'S' is only permitted if the atom passes the full tetrahedral chiral-centre test (isChiralCenter(molecule)). Passing null to clear the designation is always allowed regardless of eligibility.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#setHybridization
list.description: Sets the stored hybridization assignment on this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#setRadical
list.description: Sets the explicit radical count on this atom.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#tags
list.description: Arbitrary tags for application use.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#uuid
list.description: Universally unique identifier, auto-generated on construction.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#visible
list.description: Whether the atom participates in 2D layout and rendering. false suppresses the atom from coordinate generation and drawing (e.g. implicit H atoms); has no effect in force-graph mode.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#x
list.description: X coordinate in Å; null until generateCoords is called.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#y
list.description: Y coordinate in Å; null until generateCoords is called.

list.title: module:core/Atom.Atom#z
list.description: Z coordinate in Å; null until generateCoords is called.

list.title: module:core/Atom~_cipEqual
list.description: Returns true when the two CIP hierarchy arrays represent identical priority subtrees (i.e. the two substituents are indistinguishable by CIP).

list.title: module:core/Atom~_cipHierarchy
list.description: Builds a CIP priority hierarchy for the substituent subtree rooted at startId, treating excludeId (the chiral centre) as the boundary. Multiple bonds contribute phantom duplicate atoms per the CIP rules.

list.title: module:core/Atom~_cipZ
list.description: CIP priority key for an atom: Z * 1000 + round(massNumber). Mirrors the identical function in Molecule.js; duplicated here to avoid a circular import (Molecule.js already imports Atom.js).

list.title: module:core/Atom~_isTetrahedralCenter
list.description: Returns true when atom qualifies as a tetrahedral chiral centre in mol based on graph topology and CIP priority analysis.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#_nextId
list.description: Monotonically increasing counter used for auto-generated IDs.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#bondedTo
list.description: Returns true if atomId is one of the two atoms in this bond.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#connects
list.description: Returns true if this bond connects the two given atom IDs (in either order).

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#getAtomObjects
list.description: Returns the two Atom instances connected by this bond. Returns [null, null] for either atom that is not found in the molecule.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#getOrder
list.description: Returns the bond order. Convenience accessor for this.properties.order.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#getOtherAtom
list.description: Given one atom ID that participates in this bond, returns the other. Returns null if atomId is not one of the bond's atoms.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#getPiOrder
list.description: Returns the nominal pi-bond contribution of this bond: order - 1. Examples: single bonds return 0, double bonds 1, triple bonds 2, and aromatic 1.5-order bonds return 0.5.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#getStereo
list.description: Returns the directional stereo marker stored on this bond: '/', '\\', or null.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#hasStereo
list.description: Returns true when this bond carries a directional stereo annotation ('/' or '\\').

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#isInRing
list.description: Returns true when this bond is part of a ring. A bond is in a ring if and only if both of its endpoint atoms are in a ring AND they share at least two independent paths (detected by temporarily removing this bond and checking reachability).

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#isRotatable
list.description:
Returns true when this bond is rotatable by the standard cheminformatics definition used by this codebase: a single, non-aromatic, non-ring bond between two non-terminal heavy atoms, excluding conjugated amide-like bonds.

"Non-terminal" means the atom has at least one other heavy-atom neighbor besides the atom it shares this bond with.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#setAromatic
list.description:
Sets whether this bond is aromatic and returns this for chaining.

The two fields are kept in sync automatically:

  • setAromatic(true) sets order to 1.5 (resonance-averaged).
  • setAromatic(false) sets order to 1 (single bond).

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#setOrder
list.description: Sets the bond order and returns this for chaining. Automatically clears the aromatic flag when an integer order is set, because an explicit integer order unambiguously describes a localised bond.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#setStereo
list.description: Sets the directional stereo marker on this bond.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#tags
list.description: Arbitrary tags for application use.

list.title: module:core/Bond.Bond#uuid
list.description: Universally unique identifier, auto-generated on construction.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#_nextId
list.description: Monotonically increasing counter used for auto-generated IDs.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#addAtom
list.description: Adds an atom to the molecule.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#addBond
list.description: Adds a bond between two atoms.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#assignHybridizations
list.description:
Assigns a hybridization state to every atom in the molecule and returns this for chaining.

Rules (applied to the heavy atoms of organic/main-group chemistry):

  • 'sp' — atom has a triple bond, or two cumulated double bonds (allene center).
  • 'sp2' — atom has one double bond, or is aromatic.
  • 'sp3' — atom has only single bonds (including isolated atoms).
  • null — transition metals (groups 3–12), noble gases (group 18), s-block metals (groups 1–2, excluding H), and any atom whose element group is unknown (0 / not yet resolved).

H atoms are always assigned 'sp3'.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#changeAtomElement
list.description: Changes the element of an existing atom, updating all periodic-table derived properties (group, period, protons, neutrons, electrons). Non-element properties such as charge, aromatic, stereo, and 2D coordinates are preserved unchanged.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#clearStereoAnnotations
list.description: Clears stored stereo annotations on the specified atoms and any bonds attached to them. This is useful after interactive graph edits where the original stereochemistry annotation may no longer be trustworthy.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#clone
list.description: Returns a deep copy of this molecule. All atoms and bonds are duplicated with their IDs, UUIDs, tags, and properties preserved. The returned molecule receives a fresh auto-generated ID and UUID.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#computeAtomCharge
list.description: Computes the formal charge of a single atom from its element identity and the sum of its bond orders. Delegates to {@link Atom#computeCharge}.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#findAtom
list.description: Returns the first atom that satisfies the predicate, or null if none match.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#findBond
list.description: Returns the first bond that satisfies the predicate, or null if none match.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#findFirstSubgraphMapping
list.description: Returns the first injective mapping from query atom IDs to atom IDs in this molecule, or null if no such mapping exists.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#findSMARTS
list.description: Generator that yields all mappings from the SMARTS pattern into this molecule. Each yielded value is a Map<queryAtomId, targetAtomId>.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#findSubgraphMappings
list.description:
Generator that lazily yields every injective mapping from query atom IDs to atom IDs in this molecule. Each value is a Map<queryAtomId, targetAtomId>.

Pull values with for…of or .next() to control how many mappings are enumerated.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#firstSMARTS
list.description: Returns the first mapping from the SMARTS pattern into this molecule, or null if no match exists. The mapping is a Map<queryAtomId, targetAtomId>.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getBond
list.description: Returns the bond connecting atomIdA and atomIdB, or null if none exists.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getCharge
list.description: Computes the total formal charge of the molecule (sum of all atom charges). Automatically stored in this.properties.charge after every atom/bond mutation.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getChiralCenters
list.description: Returns the IDs of all atoms that carry a chirality annotation ('@' or '@@').

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getComponents
list.description: Returns an array of sub-Molecule instances, one per connected component. Disconnected structures (e.g. ionic pairs) are split into separate molecules.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getDegree
list.description: Returns the degree (number of bonds) of an atom.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getEZStereo
list.description:
Derives the CIP E/Z designation of a double bond from the directional (//\\) bonds attached to its two sp2 atoms.

Uses CIP priority rules: for each sp2 atom the normalised direction of its stereo-marked neighbour is corrected by flipping when that neighbour has lower CIP priority than the other substituent (so that the comparison always reflects the higher-priority group on each end).

Same corrected directions → Z; different → E.

Returns null when the bond is not a double bond or either sp2 end lacks a directional neighbour bond.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getFormula
list.description: Computes the molecular formula as a map of element symbol → count, with keys ordered by CHNOPS convention (C, H, N, O, P, S first, then remaining elements alphabetically).

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getMass
list.description:
Computes the molecular mass (g/mol).

Uses atom.properties.protons + atom.properties.neutrons when set by {@link parseSMILES} (including isotope adjustments). Falls back to standard atomic masses from the periodic table for manually-built atoms.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getName
list.description: Returns the CHNOPS-ordered formula string (C, H, N, O, P, S first, then remaining elements alphabetically).

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getNeighbors
list.description: Returns the neighbour atom IDs of a given atom.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getPath
list.description: Returns the ordered list of atom IDs on the shortest path between two atoms, or null if no path exists (disconnected graph).

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getRings
list.description:
Returns the fundamental cycle basis of the molecular graph as an array of atom-ID arrays. Each array lists the atom IDs that form one ring, in the order they were discovered by BFS.

The number of rings equals the cyclomatic number E − V + C, where C is the number of connected components.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#getSubgraph
list.description:
Returns a new Molecule containing only the atoms in atomIds and the bonds that connect them within that set.

Atom and bond IDs are preserved; the returned molecule gets a fresh ID and UUID. Bonds that cross the boundary (connect an included atom to an excluded atom) are omitted.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#hideHydrogens
list.description: Marks all explicit hydrogen atoms as invisible (atom.visible = false) without removing them from the graph. The molecule structure is preserved so that stereo bonds to H (wedge/dash) can still be rendered. Returns this.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#isAtomInRing
list.description: Tests whether the atom with the given id is part of a ring. Delegates to {@link Atom#isInRing}.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#matchesSMARTS
list.description: Returns true if the SMARTS pattern matches some subgraph of this molecule.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#matchesSubgraph
list.description:
Returns true if query is isomorphic to some subgraph of this molecule.

A thin wrapper around {@link findSubgraphMappings} that stops after the first match.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#merge
list.description: Returns a new Molecule that contains all atoms and bonds from both this molecule and other. The two molecules remain disconnected in the result unless the caller later adds bonds between them.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#name
list.description: Hill notation formula string, set by parseSMILES or getName().

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#neutralizeCharge
list.description: Sets the formal charge of every atom to 0 and recomputes all molecular properties. Returns this for chaining.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#querySMARTS
list.description: Returns all mappings from the SMARTS pattern into this molecule as an array. Convenience wrapper around findSMARTS.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#removeAtom
list.description: Removes an atom and all its connected bonds from the molecule.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#removeBond
list.description: Removes a bond from the molecule. By default, any atom that becomes isolated (degree 0) after the removal is also deleted. Pass { pruneIsolated: false } to keep isolated atoms. All computed properties (charge, formula, mass, name) are recomputed afterwards.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#repairImplicitHydrogens
list.description: Rebuilds implicit hydrogens on the specified heavy atoms without changing their existing coordinates. Hidden H atoms created during repair inherit their parent atom's current position so local edits do not force relayout.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#setAtomCharge
list.description: Sets the formal charge on an atom and recomputes all molecular properties. Delegates to {@link Atom#setCharge}, then calls _recomputeProperties.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#setAtomRadical
list.description: Sets the explicit radical count on an atom and recomputes all molecular properties.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#stripHydrogens
list.description: Returns a new Molecule with all explicit hydrogen atoms (and their bonds) removed. All heavy atoms and the bonds between them are preserved. IDs, UUIDs, tags, and properties of retained atoms/bonds are kept intact.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#tags
list.description: Arbitrary tags for application use.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#updateBond
list.description: Updates properties on an existing bond and recomputes all molecular properties.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.Molecule#uuid
list.description: Universally unique identifier, auto-generated on construction.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.assignCIPRanks
list.description: Assigns CIP priority ranks to the neighbours of centerId. Returns a parallel array of ranks (1 = lowest). Ties receive the same rank.

list.title: module:core/Molecule.computeRS
list.description: Computes the CIP R/S designation for a tetrahedral chiral center.

list.title: module:core/Molecule~_cipZ
list.description:
CIP priority key for an atom, encoding both atomic number (Rule 1) and mass number (Rule 2: same-Z atoms ranked by descending mass).

Encoding: key = Z * 1000 + massNumber Z ≤ 118, massNumber ≤ ~300, so keys stay well within safe-integer range. For atoms without an explicit isotope the standard mass is used, which keeps all non-labelled atoms of the same element at equal priority.

list.title: module:core/Molecule~_hybridizationOf
list.description: Computes the hybridization state of a single atom based on its bonds.

list.title: module:data/elements~elements
list.description:
Periodic table element data.

Each entry maps an element symbol to its atomic properties:

  • protons : atomic number
  • neutrons : average neutron count (standard atomic weight − protons); for radioactive elements with no stable isotopes the mass number of the longest-lived isotope is used instead.
  • electrons : number of electrons (equals protons for neutral atom)
  • group : periodic table column (1–18); La, Lu, Ac, Lr are d-block (group 3); inner f-block elements (Ce–Yb, Th–No) use group 0.
  • period : periodic table row (1–7)

list.title: module:data/molecule-catalog.findMolecules
list.description:
Search molecule entries across all collections.

Matches against molecule id, name, aliases, tags, smiles, inchi, and the parent collection id, name, and tags.

list.title: module:data/molecule-catalog.getMoleculeCatalogById
list.description: Find a molecule collection by its stable collection id.

list.title: module:descriptors/information.graphEntropy
list.description:
Computes the graph entropy based on vertex degree partitioning (Mowshowitz 1968).

H = -Σ (nk/n) * log2(nk/n), where nk = number of atoms with degree k.

list.title: module:descriptors/information.topologicalEntropy
list.description:
Computes the topological information index based on distance sums (Bonchev-Trinajstić).

Uses the row sums of the distance matrix as the partition criterion.

list.title: module:descriptors/molecular.molecularFormula
list.description: Computes the molecular formula as a map of element symbol → atom count. Delegates to {@link Molecule#getFormula}.

list.title: module:descriptors/molecular.molecularMass
list.description: Computes the molecular mass (g/mol). Delegates to {@link Molecule#getMass}.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.aromaticRingCount
list.description: Returns the number of fully-aromatic rings.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.formalCharge
list.description: Returns the total formal charge of the molecule.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.fsp3
list.description: Computes the fraction of sp3 carbons (Fsp3), a measure of molecular complexity and three-dimensionality.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.hBondAcceptors
list.description: Counts hydrogen-bond acceptors (all N and O atoms, Lipinski definition).

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.hBondDonors
list.description: Counts hydrogen-bond donors (NH and OH groups).

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.lipinskiRuleOfFive
list.description:
Evaluates Lipinski's Rule of Five for oral drug-likeness.

Returns an object with individual descriptor values, the number of violations, and a passes boolean (Lipinski allows one violation).

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.logP
list.description: Estimates lipophilicity using the Crippen atom-contribution method.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.molarRefractivity
list.description: Estimates the molar refractivity using the Crippen atom-contribution method (Wildman & Crippen, JCICS 1999).

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.qed
list.description:
Computes an approximate QED (Quantitative Estimate of Drug-likeness).

Uses ADS desirability functions for MW and logP (exact Bickerton 2012 parameters) and Gaussian-bell approximations for HBD, HBA, TPSA, rotatable bonds, and aromatic rings. The weighted geometric mean follows Bickerton et al. (Nat. Chem. 2012, 4, 90-98).

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.ringCount
list.description: Returns the total number of rings (SSSR).

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.rotatableBondCount
list.description: Counts rotatable bonds (single, non-aromatic, between two non-terminal heavy atoms). Delegates to {@link Bond#isRotatable}.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.stereocenters
list.description: Returns the number of defined stereocenters.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.tpsa
list.description: Computes the Topological Polar Surface Area (Å²) using Ertl atomic contributions. Only N, O, S, and P atoms contribute.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical.veberRules
list.description: Evaluates Veber's oral bioavailability rules: TPSA ≤ 140 Å² AND rotatable bonds ≤ 10.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical~VALENCE
list.description: Standard valences for implicit-H calculation.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical~_hybSp
list.description: Infer hybridisation from bond orders. Returns 'sp', 'sp2', or 'sp3'. Falls back to the stored properties.hybridization if set.

list.title: module:descriptors/physicochemical~_implicitH
list.description:
Count the implicit H atoms attached to atom using the SMILES valence rule: implicitH = primaryValence − Σ(bond orders) − |formal charge adjustment|.

Returns 0 for elements not in the valence table or when the atom is over-bonded.

list.title: module:descriptors/spectral.adjacencySpectrum
list.description: Computes the eigenvalues of the adjacency matrix, sorted in descending order.

list.title: module:descriptors/spectral.estradaIndex
list.description:
Computes the Estrada index.

EE = Σ e^λᵢ for all eigenvalues λᵢ of the adjacency matrix.

list.title: module:descriptors/spectral.laplacianSpectrum
list.description:
Computes the eigenvalues of the Laplacian matrix, sorted in ascending order.

The second-smallest eigenvalue (algebraic connectivity / Fiedler value) indicates graph connectivity strength.

list.title: module:descriptors/spectral.spectralRadius
list.description: Returns the spectral radius (largest eigenvalue of the adjacency matrix).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.abcIndex
list.description:
Computes the Atom-Bond Connectivity (ABC) index.

ABC = Σ √((deg(i) + deg(j) − 2) / (deg(i) · deg(j))) for each edge (i,j).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.balabanIndex
list.description:
Computes the Balaban J index.

J = (m / (m - n + 2)) * Σ (s_i * s_j)^(-1/2) for each edge (i,j), where s_i is the row sum of D for atom i, n = atom count, m = bond count.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.eccentricConnectivityIndex
list.description:
Computes the Eccentric Connectivity index.

ξ = Σ deg(i) · ecc(i), where ecc(i) = max_j D[i][j] (eccentricity of vertex i).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.forgottenIndex
list.description:
Computes the Forgotten index (F-index / third Zagreb index).

F = Σ deg(i)³.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.gaIndex
list.description:
Computes the Geometric-Arithmetic (GA) index.

GA = Σ 2√(deg(i) · deg(j)) / (deg(i) + deg(j)) for each edge (i,j).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.gutmanIndex
list.description:
Computes the Gutman index.

Gut = Σ_{i<j} deg(i) · deg(j) · D[i][j].

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.hararyIndex
list.description: Computes the Harary index H = (1/2) * Σ RD[i][j].

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.harmonicIndex
list.description:
Computes the Harmonic index.

H = Σ 2 / (deg(i) + deg(j)) for each edge (i,j).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.hosoyaIndex
list.description:
Computes the Hosoya Z index (total number of matchings in the graph), including the empty matching.

Uses a branching recursion: for the first remaining vertex v, either exclude it from the matching or pair it with one of its available neighbours.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.hyperWienerIndex
list.description: Computes the Hyper-Wiener index WW = (1/2) * Σ (D[i][j] + D[i][j]²) for i < j.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.narumiKatayamaIndex
list.description:
Computes the Narumi-Katayama index.

NK = ∏ deg(i) (product of all vertex degrees).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.plattIndex
list.description:
Computes the Platt index F = Σ (deg(i) + deg(j) − 2) for each edge (i,j).

Equivalently, F = M1 − 2m where M1 is the first Zagreb index and m is the number of edges.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.randicIndex
list.description: Computes the Randić connectivity index χ = Σ (deg(i) * deg(j))^(-1/2) for each edge.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.schultzIndex
list.description:
Computes the Schultz index (Molecular Topological Index, MTI).

MTI = Σ_{i<j} (deg(i) + deg(j)) · D[i][j].

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.sumConnectivityIndex
list.description:
Computes the Sum-Connectivity index.

χ_s = Σ 1 / √(deg(i) + deg(j)) for each edge (i,j).

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.szegedIndex
list.description:
Computes the Szeged index Sz = Σ n_u(e) × n_v(e) over all edges e = (u,v), where n_u(e) is the number of vertices strictly closer to u than to v, and n_v(e) is the number strictly closer to v. Vertices equidistant to both are excluded from both counts.

For acyclic graphs (trees) Sz equals the Wiener index.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.wienerIndex
list.description: Computes the Wiener index W = Σ D[i][j] for i < j.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.wienerPolarityIndex
list.description:
Computes the Wiener Polarity index.

Wₚ = number of pairs of vertices (i,j) with D[i][j] = 3.

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.zagreb1
list.description: Computes the first Zagreb index M1 = Σ deg(i)².

list.title: module:descriptors/topological.zagreb2
list.description: Computes the second Zagreb index M2 = Σ deg(i) * deg(j) for each edge (i,j).

list.title: module:io/inchi.parseINCHI
list.description:
Parses an InChI string and returns a Molecule.

Layers parsed:

  • Formula (always required) — creates heavy atoms in canonical order
  • /c connection — creates heavy-atom bonds
  • /h hydrogen — attaches explicit H atoms
  • /q charge — sets net molecular charge
  • /t /m tetrahedral stereo — assigns atom R/S where determinable
  • /b alkene stereo — assigns bond E/Z via directional bond markers

Relative/racemic stereo flags (/s values other than the absolute standard cases) and isotope (/i) layers are still ignored.

list.title: module:io/inchi.parseINCHI~fixDelocalisedChargedRings
list.description: For all-carbon aromatic rings with a non-zero net charge (e.g. tropylium C7H7+, cyclopentadienyl C5H5-), the 1.5-order aromatic bonds give each C a totalBO of 4, so assignComponentFormalCharges computes 0 for every atom and skips the component. This function assigns the net charge to a single atom — the one with the fewest H neighbours (or the first atom in tie cases) — after the standard pass has run.

list.title: module:io/inchi.toInChI
list.description:
Serialises a Molecule to a standard InChI string (formula, connection, hydrogen, and charge layers).

The returned string always starts with InChI=1S/. Layers are omitted when they carry no information (e.g. no /c for single-atom components, no /h when there are no hydrogens, no /q for neutral molecules).

For disconnected molecules (salts, mixtures) each layer is semicolon-separated by component; components are ordered largest-first (most heavy atoms), then alphabetically by Hill formula.

list.title: module:io/inchi~expandAtomList
list.description: Expands an InChI atom-list string into an array of 1-based atom indices. Supports single numbers ("1"), ranges ("1-6"), and comma-lists ("1,5,3-4").

list.title: module:io/inchi~inferBondOrders
list.description:
Infers bond orders for a molecule built from an InChI connection table.

Phase A — aromatic detection (before any bond promotion) For each heavy-atom bond, the shortest cycle through that bond is found (ignoring H atoms). 6-membered rings are aromatic when every atom has exactly one unit of unsatisfied valence (remaining = 1). 5-membered rings use the 4+1 rule: exactly 4 pi-contributor atoms (remaining = 1) plus exactly 1 lone-pair-donor heteroatom (non-C, remaining = 0), covering pyrrole, furan, thiophene, imidazole, indole, etc. Using per-bond shortest-cycle search (rather than the fundamental-cycle basis) correctly handles fused ring systems such as naphthalene and indole where the cycle basis alone would miss one of the rings.

Phase B — greedy promotion For every remaining heavy-atom bond where both endpoints have unsatisfied valence, the bond order is incremented by 1. Bonds where either endpoint belongs to an aromatic ring are skipped to prevent exocyclic substituents (e.g. carboxyl groups on benzene) from being incorrectly promoted. This loop repeats until no further changes occur.

list.title: module:io/inchi~inferBondOrders~forHeavyBonds
list.description: Iterates over all bonds whose both endpoints are heavy atoms (in heavySet). Calls cb(bond, idA, idB) for each such bond.

list.title: module:io/inchi~inferBondOrders~isAromaticRingAtom
list.description: Returns true when atomId has at least one bond in aroBondIds.

list.title: module:io/inchi~parseChargeLayer
list.description: Parses the InChI /q (charge) layer.

list.title: module:io/inchi~parseConnectionSection
list.description:
Parses the InChI /c (connection) layer into a deduplicated array of [atomA, atomB] bond pairs (1-based indices).

Grammar (recursive descent): chain := NUMBER ( '-' NUMBER | '(' branches ')' | bare_digit )* branches := chain ( ',' chain )* bare_digit := NUMBER (implicit bond after a closing ')')

Ring closures are handled naturally: a repeated atom number simply adds another bond to the already-placed atom.

list.title: module:io/inchi~parseFormula
list.description: Parses an InChI formula string into an ordered array of heavy-atom element symbols (1-indexed; index 0 is a null placeholder).

list.title: module:io/inchi~parseFormulaComponents
list.description:
Parses an InChI formula string into ordered heavy-atom components.

Component order is preserved from the formula layer so semicolon-separated layers such as /h...;... can be mapped onto the correct disconnected components. Within each component, heavy atoms follow Hill order without H.

list.title: module:io/inchi~parseHydrogenLayer
list.description:
Parses the InChI /h (hydrogen) layer into fixed assignments plus mobile hydrogen groups.

Format examples: "1-6H" atoms 1–6, 1 H each "1H3,2H2,3H" atom 1→3H, atom 2→2H, atom 3→1H "1,5H2" atoms 1 and 5, 2 H each "(H,11,12)" mobile/exchangeable H shared between listed atoms

list.title: module:io/inchi~smallestRingForBond
list.description:
Returns the atoms forming the smallest cycle that passes through bond, or null if the bond is a bridge (not in any ring).

Runs a BFS from one endpoint to the other, excluding the bond itself. The returned array does NOT repeat the start/end atom.

list.title: module:io/json.fromJSON
list.description: Deserialises a molecule from a JSON string produced by {@link toJSON}.

list.title: module:io/json.toJSON
list.description: Serialises a molecule to a plain JSON string.

list.title: module:io/smiles.decode
list.description:
Converts a token list (from {@link tokenize}) into v1-format atoms and bonds.

Returns the raw v1 graph structure, not a {@link Molecule} instance. Use {@link parseSMILES} to get a Molecule.

list.title: module:io/smiles.parseSMILES
list.description:
Parses a SMILES string and returns a {@link Molecule}.

Internally uses the v1 {@link tokenize} and {@link decode} pipeline, then converts the result into a Molecule. Each Atom gains extra periodic-table properties: protons, neutrons, electrons, group, period.

list.title: module:io/smiles.toCanonicalSMILES
list.description:
Serializes a {@link Molecule} to a canonical SMILES string.

Atom traversal order is determined by the Morgan extended-connectivity algorithm (Weininger 1989), so the same molecular graph always produces the same string regardless of how the molecule was constructed or which input SMILES was parsed. The output is therefore suitable as a deduplication key or database identifier.

Disconnected components are each canonicalized independently and then sorted lexicographically before being joined with '.'.

All features of {@link toSMILES} are preserved: chirality (@/@@), E/Z geometry (//\\), isotopes, charges, aromatic atoms, ring closures.

list.title: module:io/smiles.toSMILES
list.description:
Serializes a {@link Molecule} to a SMILES string.

The output is non-canonical (traversal follows internal atom insertion order) but is valid SMILES that round-trips through {@link parseSMILES} to a chemically equivalent molecule.

Supported features

  • Organic-subset atoms with implicit H (B, C, N, O, P, S, F, Cl, Br, I)

  • Bracket atoms: explicit charge, H count, and isotope mass number

  • Ring systems (ring-closure numbers 1–99+, %nn notation)

  • Branch notation

  • Double / triple / quadruple bonds

  • Aromatic atoms and bonds (lowercase symbols, implicit :)

  • Disconnected molecules as dot-separated components

  • Tetrahedral chirality (@ / @@) for atoms with a stored CIP designation (properties.chirality === 'R' or 'S')

  • E/Z geometry (/ / \\) on bonds that carry a stored stereo direction

list.title: module:io/smiles.tokenize
list.description: Parses a SMILES string into an array of tokens using the v1 grammar.

list.title: module:io/smiles~ORGANIC_VALENCE
list.description: Normal SMILES valence for each organic-subset element (lowest standard valence).

list.title: module:io/smiles~_atomToken
list.description:
Builds the SMILES atom token for atom.

Returns a bare element symbol when the atom is in the organic subset, has no charge, no non-standard isotope, and the SMILES implicit-H rule would assign exactly pendantHCount hydrogens. Otherwise returns a bracket atom (e.g. [NH4+], [13CH4], [nH]).

list.title: module:io/smiles~_bondToken
list.description:
Returns the SMILES bond character for bond. Single bonds (order 1) and aromatic bonds both return '' (implicit).

When fromId is supplied and the bond has a directional stereo property ('/' or '\\'), returns the direction relative to fromId as the source atom (flipping when fromId is bond.atoms[1]).

list.title: module:io/smiles~_isStrippable
list.description: Returns true when atom is a standard pendant hydrogen that can be represented implicitly in SMILES output (uncharged, mass-number 1, pendant to exactly one non-H atom).

list.title: module:io/smiles~_ringToken
list.description: Formats a ring-closure integer as its SMILES token: single digits 1–9 are written bare; 10+ use %nn notation.

list.title: module:io/smiles~_serializeComponent
list.description: Serialises a single connected Molecule component into a SMILES string.

list.title: module:io/smiles~extractChiralNeighborOrders
list.description:
Builds a map from clean atom ID → SMILES chirality neighbour order for every @/@@ atom in the SMILES string.

The clean atom ID scheme mirrors what decode()'s internal clean() step produces: atoms are numbered sequentially by ascending character position, and each gets the ID <elementSymbol><rank> (e.g. C1, H3, N4).

The SMILES chirality neighbour order is: 0. the "from" atom (the last atom token before the opening [)

  1. the bracket H (the first atom token inside […], if any) 2+ remaining neighbours in left-to-right SMILES string order (first atom of each (…) branch, then the chain continuation)

Ring-closure bonds at the chiral centre are handled: both ring tokens embedded inside the bracket and depth-0 ring closures after ] are inserted into the neighbour list in SMILES reading order. Chain-start atoms (no preceding atom) are also supported.

list.title: module:io/smiles~findFromAtomPos
list.description:
Scans backward from bracketOpenPos - 1 to find the position of the "from" atom: the atom immediately before the [ of a bracket atom in the SMILES chain, correctly skipping (…) branches and treating […] bracket atoms as single units.

Returns the character index of the from-atom, or -1 if none (chain start).

list.title: module:io/smiles~findRingTokenAtomPos
list.description:
Returns the character position of the atom that owns a ring token.

Ring tokens sometimes include the atom text inside the token span ([C@H]1, C=1) and sometimes start at the digit immediately after the atom (C1, [C@@H]4). Prefer an atom token that falls inside the ring token span, then fall back to the nearest atom token immediately before the ring token.

list.title: module:io/smiles~previousAtomSkipBranches
list.description: Like previousAtom but skips over (…) branch groups when scanning backward. Used to find the true source atom for stereo bonds that follow a branch close ).

list.title: module:io/smiles~ringTokenBondOrder
list.description: Extracts the explicit bond order embedded in a ring token term (e.g. 'C=1' → 2). Returns null if no bond prefix is present.

list.title: module:layout.generateAndRefine2dCoords
list.description: Generate 2D coordinates for mol then refine them.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d.generateCoords
list.description: Assigns 2D coordinates to every atom in molecule. Coordinates are written to atom.x and atom.y.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d.refineExistingCoords
list.description:
Refines already assigned 2D coordinates by trying discrete subtree moves around rotatable single bonds. This is intended as a conservative cleanup pass after an initial layout, not as a replacement for generateCoords().

Reads atom.x / atom.y, mutates them in place when an improving move is found, and returns the final coordinate map.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~SpatialGrid#hasNear
list.description: Returns true if any atom (other than those in exclude) is within thresh of (x,y).

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~SpatialGrid.fromCoords
list.description: Build from an existing coords map (all entries).

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~_idealAngle
list.description: Returns the ideal interior bond angle (radians) for an atom based on its degree and bond orders (sp / sp2 / sp3 hybridisation).

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~_reflectPoint
list.description: Reflects point p across the line through a and b.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~_separateOverlappingComponents
list.description: If molecule has multiple disconnected components whose bounding boxes overlap (or nearly touch), re-arranges them in a horizontal row with bondLength * 2 gaps between them, vertically centred at y = 0. Returns the (possibly updated) coords map; the input map is never mutated.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~centroid
list.description: Centroid of a set of atom IDs from a coords map.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~circumradius
list.description: Circumradius of a regular n-gon with side length s. R = s / (2·sin(π/n)).

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~computeChildAngles
list.description: Computes outgoing angles for n child atoms from a parent atom.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~countClashes
list.description: Returns the number of proposed child positions that are too close to any already-placed atom. Uses a SpatialGrid for O(1) lookup when provided, otherwise falls back to a linear scan.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~detectRingSystems
list.description: Groups the output of molecule.getRings() into ring systems. Two rings belong to the same system if they share at least one atom.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~findSharedAtoms
list.description: Returns atom IDs shared between two rings (as arrays).

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~forceFieldRefine
list.description:
2D molecular-mechanics force-field relaxation, inspired by ChemDraw's "Clean Up Structure" algorithm.

Three pairwise potentials are applied in each gradient-descent step:

  1. Bond-length springs – harmonic spring along every bond, restoring to bondLength.
  2. Bond-angle bending – for every atom with ≥2 neighbours, a harmonic spring on each bond-angle pair, restoring to the ideal angle (180° for sp, 120° everywhere else in a 2D depiction).
  3. Non-bonded repulsion – soft-wall (1/r³) repulsion between every pair of heavy atoms that are not bonded (1-2) or angle-neighbours (1-3), acting inside a distance cut-off of 2.2 × bondLength.

Ring atoms (frozen) are held fixed throughout. The integrator runs up to MAX_ITER damped gradient-descent steps; it exits early once the maximum per-atom displacement drops below CONVERGE.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~layoutChain
list.description: Depth-first chain layout from startAtomId. The atom at startAtomId must already have coordinates. Uses an explicit stack (iterative) to avoid call-stack overflows on large molecules, and a SpatialGrid for O(1) clash detection.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~layoutComponents
list.description: Lays out each connected component separately and tiles them horizontally.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~levelCoords
list.description:
Applies a corrective rotation so that bond directions align as closely as possible to the natural angular grid of each bond's ring system.

Each bond carries its own grid increment:

  • Ring bond in a ring of size n → π / n (e.g. 30° for 6-ring, 36° for 5-ring)
  • Bond shared by multiple rings → minimum of the containing rings' grids (the most restrictive constraint wins)
  • Chain bond (no ring) → π / 6 (30°, standard zigzag)

The optimal rotation is found by evaluating every candidate that would snap at least one bond exactly onto its grid, then selecting the one that minimises the total squared deviation across all bonds. This is exact and works for any ring size or mixture of ring sizes without special-casing.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~normalizeAngle
list.description: Normalize angle to [−π, π].

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~normalizeOrientation
list.description:
Rotates all atoms in coords so the principal axis (direction of maximum spatial spread, computed via the 2D inertia tensor) is horizontal.

Only applied when there are ≥2 heavy atoms with distinct positions. Acyclic chains already have a natural horizontal layout from the DFS zigzag and must NOT be rotated (their bond-length exactness depends on the fixed geometry). So the caller is responsible for only invoking this when appropriate (currently: ring-containing molecules only).

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~placeHydrogens
list.description: Places explicit pendant H atoms (degree 1, element 'H') near their heavy-atom parent. H atoms are placed radially away from the parent's other neighbors.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~placeRing
list.description: Places atoms of a single ring. Small rings (≤ 8 atoms): regular polygon. Large rings (> 8 atoms): rectangular perimeter walk — atoms are placed at exact bondLength intervals along the four edges of a rectangle, keeping all ring bonds at the correct length. The rectangle aspect ratio is chosen to approximate the golden ratio.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~placeRingSystem
list.description: Places a ring system (one or more fused/spiro rings). Modifies coords in-place. All placed atom IDs are added to placed.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~pointInPolygon
list.description: Ray-casting point-in-polygon test. Returns true if point p is strictly inside the polygon defined by vertices.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~pointToSegmentDistance
list.description: Shortest distance from point p to line segment ab.

list.title: module:layout/coords2d~refineCoords
list.description:
Analytic geometry refinement pass.

For every ring attachment atom (in frozen with ≥1 non-ring substituent), recomputes substituent positions analytically:

  1. Gather the exact angles to its ring-neighbour(s) from coords.
  2. Find the largest angular gap around the ring atom.
  3. Distribute the substituents evenly within that gap.
  4. Re-run layoutChain on each substituent's subtree from the new position.

Ring atoms (in frozen) are never moved.

list.title: module:matrices.adjacencyMatrix
list.description:
Builds the adjacency matrix of a molecule (hydrogen-suppressed).

Hydrogen atoms are excluded; only heavy atoms appear as rows/columns. A[i][j] = 1 if heavy atoms i and j share a bond, 0 otherwise. Atoms are indexed in insertion order.

list.title: module:matrices.allMatrices
list.description: Computes all standard matrices for a molecule in a single pass.

list.title: module:matrices.degreeMatrix
list.description:
Builds the degree matrix from an adjacency matrix.

DEG is a diagonal matrix where DEG[i][i] = sum of row i in A (the vertex degree).

list.title: module:matrices.distanceMatrix
list.description:
Builds the distance matrix from an adjacency matrix using Floyd-Warshall.

D[i][j] = shortest path length between atom i and atom j.

list.title: module:matrices.laplacianMatrix
list.description: Builds the Laplacian matrix L = DEG - A.

list.title: module:matrices.randicMatrix
list.description:
Builds the Randić matrix.

R[i][j] = 1 / sqrt(deg(i) * deg(j)) if atoms i and j are bonded, 0 otherwise.

list.title: module:matrices.reciprocalMatrix
list.description:
Builds the reciprocal distance matrix.

RD[i][j] = 1 / D[i][j] for i ≠ j, 0 for i = j.

list.title: module:smarts/parser.parseSMARTS
list.description:
Parses a SMARTS string into a query Molecule.

Each atom in the returned molecule has a _predicate property: (tAtom, targetMol) => boolean

Each bond in the returned molecule has a _predicate property: (tBond) => boolean

These predicates are consumed by {@link findSMARTS} when it constructs the atomMatch / bondMatch closures passed to VF2.

Supported syntax:

  • Bracket atoms: [C], [#6], [NH2], [C;!R], [C,N], [+1], …
  • Bare atoms: C, c, N, n, O, o, S, s, P, Cl, Br, *
  • Bond tokens: -, =, #, :, @, ~, /, \
  • Branches: (…)
  • Ring closures: single digit 1–9 or %nn
  • Disconnected components: .

list.title: module:smarts/parser.parseSMARTS~addAtomNode
list.description: Creates a new query atom node with the given predicate, connects it to prevId (if any), and updates prevId.

list.title: module:smarts/parser.parseSMARTS~pendingBondPred
list.description: Pending bond predicate set by a bond token. Applied when the next atom is added. null means "use the default SMARTS bond predicate".

list.title: module:smarts/parser.parseSMARTS~prevId
list.description: ID of the most recently added atom.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives.compileAtomExpr
list.description:
Compiles a SMARTS atom expression (the text between [ and ]) into a predicate (tAtom, targetMol) => boolean.

Supported operators (highest → lowest precedence): ! NOT & or implicit AND (adjacent primitives) — high precedence ; AND — low precedence , OR

Supported primitives: * wildcard a / A aromatic / aliphatic #n atomic number n +n formal charge +n (+ alone = any positive charge) -n formal charge -n (- alone = any negative charge) Hn total H count = n (H alone = 1) Dn heavy-atom degree = n (D alone = 1) Rn in any ring when n=0 means not in ring; n>0 = in exactly n rings (SSSR) rn in a ring of exactly size n (r alone = any ring) Xn total connectivity (heavy + H bonds) = n vn valence (sum of bond orders) = n $(smarts) recursive SMARTS — requires options.parseFn Element symbol (uppercase, optionally + one lowercase) — element match Lowercase element symbol (e.g. c) — aromatic element

list.title: module:smarts/primitives.compileBareAtomToken
list.description:
Parses a bare SMARTS atom token (outside [...]) starting at smarts[pos].

Uppercase → aliphatic element (e.g. C = aliphatic carbon) Lowercase → aromatic element (e.g. c = aromatic carbon) * → any atom (wildcard)

Returns { pred, len } or null if the character is not a valid bare atom.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives.compileBondToken
list.description:
Parses a single bond primitive character at smarts[pos] and returns { pred, len, props }, where pred is a (tBond) => boolean predicate and len is the number of characters consumed.

Returns null if the character is not a bond token.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives.defaultSmartsBondPred
list.description: Default SMARTS bond predicate — matches single bonds and aromatic bonds. Applied when no explicit bond token appears between two atoms in a SMARTS.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_heavyDegree
list.description: Count of heavy (non-H) neighbors in mol.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_isAroBond
list.description:
Returns true when a bond is aromatic.

The SMILES parser may create aromatic bonds with either aromatic: true (explicit : bond token) or order: 1.5 with aromatic: false (implicit bond between lowercase aromatic atoms, including ring-closure bonds). Both representations are treated as "aromatic" by SMARTS predicates.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_isBondInRing
list.description: Returns true if bond is part of any ring in mol.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_protons
list.description: Atomic number of an atom, falling back to the elements table.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_ringPathCount
list.description:
Number of chordless rings containing atom.

This is the closest match to Daylight-style ring-path semantics for fused and bridged systems without relying on a lossy cycle basis.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_ringSizesContaining
list.description:
Returns a Set of chordless ring sizes that contain atom.

This avoids the undercounting caused by relying on a fundamental cycle basis (for example cubane faces) and the overcounting caused by naive neighbour- pair BFS across fused systems.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_totalConnectivity
list.description: Total bond count (connectivity), including bonds to H.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_totalHCount
list.description: Count of explicit H neighbors in mol.

list.title: module:smarts/primitives~_valence
list.description: Sum of bond orders (valence).

list.title: module:smarts/reference.functionalGroups
list.description:
Catalogue of common functional groups mapped to SMARTS query strings.

Each entry has: name — human-readable display name smarts — SMARTS pattern for use with matchesSMARTS, findSMARTS, etc.

list.title: module:smarts/search.findSMARTS
list.description:
Generator that yields every unique match of smarts into target.

Two mappings are considered duplicates if they cover the same set of target atom IDs (VF2 can return the same atom set multiple times via different traversal orderings, e.g. 12× per ring). Each yielded value is a Map<queryAtomId, targetAtomId>.

list.title: module:smarts/search.findSMARTSRaw
list.description:
Generator that yields every raw mapping of smarts into target, without deduping by the set of matched target atoms.

This preserves the exact query-atom-to-target-atom embeddings returned by VF2, which is useful for transform languages such as SMIRKS where different embeddings over the same atom set can lead to different products.

list.title: module:smarts/search.firstSMARTS
list.description: Returns the first mapping from smarts into target, or null if none.

list.title: module:smarts/search.firstSMARTSRaw
list.description: Returns the first raw mapping from smarts into target, or null if none.

list.title: module:smarts/search.matchesSMARTS
list.description: Returns true if smarts matches some subgraph of target.

list.title: module:smarts/search~_vf2Options
list.description:
Creates the atomMatch and bondMatch closures that wire a parsed SMARTS query molecule into the VF2 engine.

Each atom in queryMol must have a _predicate(tAtom, targetMol) → boolean property. Each bond must have a _predicate(tBond) → boolean property.

list.title: module:smirks/reference.reactionTemplates
list.description:
Catalogue of common reaction-template motifs mapped to SMIRKS strings.

These are intentionally conservative templates that fit the current phase-1 SMIRKS engine. They are useful as named starting points, not as fully balanced mechanistic reaction rules.

Each entry has: name — human-readable display name smirks — SMIRKS transform for use with parseSMIRKS / applySMIRKS

list.title: module:utils/combinatorics.binomial
list.description: Computes the binomial coefficient C(n, k).

list.title: module:utils/combinatorics.combinations
list.description: Returns all k-element combinations of an array.

list.title: module:utils/combinatorics.factorial
list.description: Computes n! (factorial).

list.title: module:utils/eigenvalues.computeEigenvalues
list.description:
Computes all eigenvalues of a real symmetric matrix using the Jacobi eigenvalue algorithm.

Iteratively zeroes the largest off-diagonal element via Givens rotations until all off-diagonal elements are below a tolerance.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.addMatrices
list.description: Adds two matrices element-wise.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.identity
list.description: Creates an n×n identity matrix.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.multiplyMatrices
list.description: Multiplies two matrices.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.ones
list.description: Creates an n×m matrix filled with ones.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.scalarMultiply
list.description: Multiplies a matrix by a scalar.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.subtractMatrices
list.description: Subtracts matrix B from A element-wise.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.transposeMatrix
list.description: Transposes a matrix.

list.title: module:utils/matrix.zeros
list.description: Creates an n×m matrix filled with zeros.

list.title: module:validation/valence.validateValence
list.description:
Validates the valence (total bond order) of each atom in a molecule.

Uses the electron-count parity rule derived from formal-charge theory:

ec = valenceElectrons(element) − formalCharge − radicalCount

allowed bond orders = non-negative integers ≤ allowedMax that share the same parity as ec

where allowedMax enforces orbital-count limits:

  • period 1 (H, He): min(ec, 2 − ec) — one s orbital, 2 e⁻ max
  • period 2 (Li–Ne): min(ec, 8 − ec) — octet rule from both ends; correctly gives C→4, N→3, O→2, F→1 for neutral atoms
  • period 3+ (Na–Xe): min(ec, 8) — d-orbital participation, generous cap

Aromatic atoms are skipped: their bond orders are resonance-averaged (fractional) and only well-defined after Kekulé assignment.

Transition metals (groups 3–12) and unknown elements are silently skipped.

Bond orders are floored before summing so that fractional aromatic bond orders stored by the SMILES parser (1.5) are treated as sigma bonds (1) for non-aromatic atoms that border aromatic rings.

list.title: perpUnit
list.description: Unit perpendicular vector (rotated 90° CCW) for a direction (dx, dy).

list.title: secondaryDir
list.description: Returns +1 or -1 indicating which side of the bond axis the secondary parallel line of a double bond should be placed on, based on the positions of neighbouring heavy atoms.

list.title: shortenLine
list.description: Shorten a line segment by d1 at the start and d2 at the end.

list.title: stereoBondTypeForCenter
list.description:
Returns a Map from bond ID → 'wedge' | 'dash' for all chiral centers in the molecule that have 2D coordinates assigned.

Picks exactly one bond per chiral center, preferring visible exocyclic substituents, then visible ring atoms, then H. Within each tier the bond with the largest vertical offset from the center is chosen (most perpendicular to the main chain). The wedge/dash type is determined via parity-aware CIP winding so the correct absolute configuration is conveyed regardless of which substituent is chosen.

list.title: vecLen
list.description: Length of 2D vector (x, y). Returns at least 1 to avoid division by zero.
